user_input.fundingSource: - Can be supported under Digital Kerala / Emerging Technologies schemes. - Possible use of existing departmental IT budget for pilot phase
user_input.Expected benefits: Time saved in locating relevant documents - Reduction in errors due to use of outdated GOs - Improved efficiency in decision-making - Single authoritative reference for all officials - Better transparency and accountability
user_input.Idea Description:
The Finance Department issues a very large number of Government Orders (GOs), circulars, 
notifications, office memoranda, and budget documents every year. While these are 
published on the Department’s website, officials face several challenges in using them 
effectively: - Difficulty in retrieval: With thousands of documents spanning multiple years, it is time
consuming to locate the exact order relevant to a case. - Version confusion: Officials sometimes rely on outdated or superseded GOs, as revised or 
withdrawn versions are not easily traceable. - Mixed formats: Older documents are available only as scanned PDFs, while newer ones 
are machine-readable. This inconsistency makes uniform searching difficult. - Lack of contextual guidance: Current systems do not indicate whether a GO is still valid, 
amended, or withdrawn, leaving scope for errors. - Operational delays: File processing and decision-making are slowed down because 
officials spend significant time verifying the correctness of documents. 
Together, these issues affect the efficiency, accuracy, and reliability of financial governance.

similar_cases.description: This advanced solution utilizes ai_ml to manage financial operations and revenue optimization, addressing key challenges in the public services domain.

similar_cases.case_name:
SSB hold vast amounts of unstructured and semi-structured information, 
majority of which is in Malayalam, spread across reports, databases, profiles, 
and archives. Currently, retrieving relevant information about a person, 
subject, or scheme requires manual search through multiple files and 
formats. This results in delays, inefficiencies, and the risk of missing critical 
insights. Officers face challenges in accessing accurate data quickly, especially 
when queries are made in Malayalam.
similar_cases.description: An automated system that employs nlp to analyze data and generate valuable business intelligence, delivering improved performance in public services management.

similar_cases.case_name:
Auto generation of reports and documents from the data available from the 
various web applications of the Board.
similar_cases.description: An automated system that employs ai_ml and big_data to manage document registration and filing processes efficiently, delivering improved performance in public services management.

Handling queries from public regarding the norms and services of the Board, 
scrutiny of applications and documents, fraud detection, compliance
